steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.Mali: 'Mali' is a noun in the genitive meaning 'of evil'
hints.sceleris: 'sceleris' is a noun in the genitive meaning 'of crime'
hints.fingi: 'fingi' is a passive infinitive meaning 'to be devised' — it complements 'potest'

hints.to be powerful: 'to be powerful' is 'pollere' — 'posse' means 'to be able'

hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'possit' — 'potest' is indicative

hints.pono, ponere, posui, positum: These are the parts of 'ponere' (to place)
hints.potior, potiri, potitus sum: These are the parts of 'potiri' (to gain possession)
hints.polliceor, polliceri, pollicitus sum: These are the parts of 'polliceri' (to promise)

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'malum' — 'Mali' with -i ending is genitive, partitive with 'quid'
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'malo' — 'Mali' is partitive genitive

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'scelus' — 'sceleris' with -is ending is genitive
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'scelere' — 'sceleris' is partitive genitive

hints.1st: 1st declension genitive ends in -ae — 'scelus, sceleris' is 3rd declension neuter
hints.2nd: 2nd declension genitive ends in -i — 'scelus, sceleris' is 3rd declension
hints.5th: 5th declension genitive ends in -ei — 'scelus, sceleris' is 3rd declension

steps.prompt: The passive infinitive 'fingi' comes from which verb?
steps.target: fingo, fingere, finxi, fictum — to invent / devise
choices: fingo, fingere, finxi, fictum — to invent / devise
figo, figere, fixi, fixum — to fix / fasten
finio, finire, finivi, finitum — to finish
fundo, fundere, fudi, fusum — to pour

translation: For what evil or crime can be devised?
